Notebook name

customer_churn_processing

Exported at 10:32 PM EDT on June 28, 2026

Customer churn data processing notebook Co-authored with CoCo

TELECOM CUSTOMER CHURN PREDICTION USING LOGISTIC REGRESSION AND XGBOOST

Import the libraries

Python[117]
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import seaborn as sn
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.preprocessing import MinMaxScaler
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
import xgboost as xgb
from xgboost import XGBClassifier
import shap
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report, accuracy_score, confusion_matrix, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score, roc_curve, roc_auc_score

Create a snowflake session and import the data

Python[118]
from snowflake.snowpark.context import get_active_session
session = get_active_session()
ccd = session.table('CUSTOMER_CHURN.TELECOM.CUSTOMER_CHURN').to_pandas()

Identifying the duplicates

Python[127]
ccd1.duplicated().sum()
np.int64(0)
Python[128]
def unique_columns(ccd):
    for column in ccd:
        if ccd[column].dtype == 'object':
            print(f'{column}:{ccd[column].unique()}')
Python[129]
unique_columns(ccd1)
CUSTOMERID:['7590-VHVEG' '5575-GNVDE' '3668-QPYBK' ... '4801-JZAZL' '8361-LTMKD'
 '3186-AJIEK']
GENDER:['Female' 'Male']
MULTIPLELINES:['No phone service' 'No' 'Yes']
INTERNETSERVICE:['DSL' 'Fiber optic' 'No']
ONLINESECURITY:['No' 'Yes' 'No internet service']
ONLINEBACKUP:['Yes' 'No' 'No internet service']
DEVICEPROTECTION:['No' 'Yes' 'No internet service']
TECHSUPPORT:['No' 'Yes' 'No internet service']
STREAMINGTV:['No' 'Yes' 'No internet service']
STREAMINGMOVIES:['No' 'Yes' 'No internet service']
CONTRACT:['Month-to-month' 'One year' 'Two year']
PAYMENTMETHOD:['Electronic check' 'Mailed check' 'Bank transfer (automatic)'
 'Credit card (automatic)']

Python[131]
yes_no_cols=['MULTIPLELINES','ONLINESECURITY','ONLINEBACKUP','DEVICEPROTECTION','TECHSUPPORT','STREAMINGTV','STREAMINGMOVIES']

Converting the yes or no type columns to 0s and 1s for ML models

Python[132]
for cols in yes_no_cols:
    ccd1[cols].replace({'Yes':1, 'No':0},inplace=True)
ccd1.dtypes
FutureWarning: A value is trying to be set on a copy of a DataFrame or Series through chained assignment using an inplace method.
The behavior will change in pandas 3.0. This inplace method will never work because the intermediate object on which we are setting values always behaves as a copy.

For example, when doing 'df[col].method(value, inplace=True)', try using 'df.method({col: value}, inplace=True)' or df[col] = df[col].method(value) instead, to perform the operation inplace on the original object.


FutureWarning: Downcasting behavior in `replace` is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. To retain the old behavior, explicitly call `result.infer_objects(copy=False)`. To opt-in to the future behavior, set `pd.set_option('future.no_silent_downcasting', True)`

CONTRACT_One year                             bool

PAYMENTMETHOD_Mailed check                    bool
dtype: object

Convert the features to the data type compatible for the ML models (yes or no and true or false types to 1s and 0s)

Python[136]
boolean_cols = [
    'SENIORCITIZEN',
    'PARTNER',
    'DEPENDENTS',
    'PHONESERVICE',
    'PAPERLESSBILLING',
    'CHURN',
    'INTERNETSERVICE_DSL',
    'INTERNETSERVICE_Fiber optic',
    'INTERNETSERVICE_No',
    'CONTRACT_Month-to-month',
    'CONTRACT_One year',
    'CONTRACT_Two year',
    'PAYMENTMETHOD_Bank transfer (automatic)',
    'PAYMENTMETHOD_Credit card (automatic)',
    'PAYMENTMETHOD_Electronic check',
    'PAYMENTMETHOD_Mailed check' 
]
for cols in boolean_cols:
    ccd2[cols] = ccd2[cols].replace({True: 1, False: 0})
ccd2.dtypes
FutureWarning: Downcasting behavior in `replace` is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. To retain the old behavior, explicitly call `result.infer_objects(copy=False)`. To opt-in to the future behavior, set `pd.set_option('future.no_silent_downcasting', True)`

Save the cleaned data

Python[137]
session.sql("USE DATABASE CUSTOMER_CHURN").collect()
session.sql("USE SCHEMA TELECOM").collect()
session.write_pandas(ccd2, 'CUSTOMER_CHURNS_transformed', auto_create_table=True, overwrite=True)

Standardize the int and float columns

Python[139]
cols_to_transform = ['TENURE','MONTHLYCHARGES','TOTALCHARGES']
scaler=StandardScaler()
ccd2[cols_to_transform]=scaler.fit_transform(ccd2[cols_to_transform])
ccd2.head(10)
10 rows
GENDER
SENIORCITIZEN
PARTNER
DEPENDENTS
TENURE
PHONESERVICE
MULTIPLELINES
ONLINESECURITY
ONLINEBACKUP
DEVICEPROTECTION
TECHSUPPORT
STREAMINGTV
STREAMINGMOVIES
PAPERLESSBILLING
MONTHLYCHARGES
TOTALCHARGES
CHURN
INTERNETSERVICE_DSL
INTERNETSERVICE_FIBER OPTIC
INTERNETSERVICE_NO
CONTRACT_MONTH-TO-MONTH
CONTRACT_ONE YEAR
CONTRACT_TWO YEAR
PAYMENTMETHOD_BANK TRANSFER (AUTOMATIC)
PAYMENTMETHOD_CREDIT CARD (AUTOMATIC)
PAYMENTMETHOD_ELECTRONIC CHECK
PAYMENTMETHOD_MAILED CHECK

Divide the data into training and testing data

Python[140]
X=ccd2.drop('CHURN',axis=1)
y=ccd2['CHURN']
X_train, X_test, y_train,y_test=train_test_split(X,y,test_size=0.3,random_state=42)
X_train.shape
(4922, 26)

Logistic Regressio Model

Python[141]
model = LogisticRegression()
model.fit(X_train,y_train)
LogisticRegression()
In a Jupyter environment, please rerun this cell to show the HTML representation or trust the notebook.
On GitHub, the HTML representation is unable to render, please try loading this page with nbviewer.org.
Parameters
penalty  'l2'
dual  False
tol  0.0001
C  1.0
fit_intercept  True
intercept_scaling  1
class_weight  None
random_state  None
solver  'lbfgs'
max_iter  100
multi_class  'deprecated'
verbose  0
warm_start  False
n_jobs  None
l1_ratio  None
Python[142]
predictions=model.predict(X_test)
predictions
array([0, 0, 1, ..., 0, 0, 0], shape=(2110,))

Classification Metrics

Python[143]
Accuracy=accuracy_score(y_test,predictions)
print(f'The accuracy is {round((Accuracy*100),2)}')
precision=precision_score(y_test,predictions)
print(f'The precision is {round((precision*100),2)}')
f1=f1_score(y_test,predictions)
print(f'The f1 score is {round((f1*100),2)}')
recall=recall_score(y_test,predictions)
print(f'The recall is {round((recall*100),2)}')
confusion_matrix=confusion_matrix(y_test,predictions)
confusion_matrix
The accuracy is 79.67
The precision is 64.1
The f1 score is 58.31
The recall is 53.48

array([[1381,  168],
       [ 261,  300]])

Confusion Matrix

Python[163]
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix as _confusion_matrix

cm = _confusion_matrix(y_test, predictions)

plt.figure(figsize = (10,7))
sn.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt='d')
plt.xlabel('Predicted')
plt.ylabel('Truth')
Text(95.72222222222221, 0.5, 'Truth')

ROC AUC curve

Python[145]
y_prob = model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

fpr, tpr, thresholds = roc_curve(y_test, y_prob)

roc_auc = roc_auc_score(y_test, y_prob)

plt.figure()
plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"ROC Curve (AUC = {roc_auc})")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--") 
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("ROC Curve")
plt.legend()
plt.show()

Similar Steps for XG boost model

Python[146]
params = {"objective":"binary:logistic",
"max_depth":4,
"learning_rate":0.1,
"n_estimators":100,
"alpha":10
}
classifier=XGBClassifier(**params)
classifier.fit(X_train,y_train)
predictionn=classifier.predict(X_test)
Python[147]
accuracy=accuracy_score(y_test,predictionn)
print(f'The accuracy is {accuracy*100}')
The accuracy is 80.09478672985783
Python[165]
from sklearn.metrics import precision_score, f1_score, recall_score, confusion_matrix as _confusion_matrix_fn

precision=precision_score(y_test,predictionn)
print(f'The precision is {round((precision*100),2)}')
f1=f1_score(y_test,predictionn)
print(f'The f1 score is {round((f1*100),2)}')
recall=recall_score(y_test,predictionn)
print(f'The recall is {round((recall*100),2)}')
cm=_confusion_matrix_fn(y_test,predictionn)
cm
The precision is 65.56
The f1 score is 58.58
The recall is 52.94

array([[1393,  156],
       [ 264,  297]])
Python[149]
print(classification_report(y_test,predictionn))
              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.84      0.90      0.87      1549
           1       0.66      0.53      0.59       561

    accuracy                           0.80      2110
   macro avg       0.75      0.71      0.73      2110
weighted avg       0.79      0.80      0.79      2110
Python[158]
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix

cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, predictionn)

plt.figure(figsize = (10,7))
sn.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt='d')
plt.xlabel('Predicted')
plt.ylabel('Truth')
Text(95.72222222222221, 0.5, 'Truth')
Python[159]
y_prob = classifier.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

fpr, tpr, thresholds = roc_curve(y_test, y_prob)

roc_auc = roc_auc_score(y_test, y_prob)

plt.figure()
plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"ROC Curve (AUC = {roc_auc})")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--") 
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("ROC Curve")
plt.legend()
plt.show()

Iterpreting the XG Boost model

Python[160]
Explainer=shap.TreeExplainer(classifier)
shap_values=Explainer(X_test)
shap.initjs()

Ranking the features that influence the target variable 'CHURN'

Python[161]
shap.summary_plot(shap_values,X_test,plot_type='bar')

CONCLUSION

Both Logistic Regression and XG boost models work the best for the churn data. But, XG boost wins by one point. XGBoost model is a esemble model also called as a black box model which means we cannot understand so easily what is the background for all the predictions made. Hence we interpret the mdoels using different interpretation methods. One of them is the SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) method which gives the ranking of the features that most influence the target variable 'Churn'. As we can see, The month to month contract, Tenure are the variables that highly influence the if the customers dropping the service or not.
